findings.summary: GET /v1/connectors/drive/connection/{id}/skipped returns 200 with empty groups for a non-existent connection id, while the parent GET /v1/connectors/drive/connection/{id} returns 404 (CONNECTION_NOT_FOUND). Inconsistent error contract; no data exposure.

GET /v1/connectors/drive/connection/{id} with non-UUID path param returns 500 with raw 'invalid input syntax for type uuid: "not-a-uuid" (SQLSTATE 22P02)' message. No injection (parameterized query rejected the cast); validate UUID at handler boundary and return 400 with stable error code.

POST /api/search with control-character query (\u0000\u0001 + injection-style suffix) returns 500 SEARCH_FAILED. Other malformed queries return clean 200 text_fallback. Strip/reject control characters at handler.

OAuth callback redirect URL reflects raw provider/database error string ('error=google%3A+lookup+oauth+state%3A+no+rows+in+result+set') to the browser. Emit stable error code (e.g. error=oauth_state_invalid) instead of leaking internal wording.

executionHistory.summary: Round 9 stabilize pass routed by bubbles.workflow to diagnose and fix the cross-cutting e2e infra flake that blocked Scope 1 DoD-8 (broader e2e regression) and DoD-12 (full pipeline) for feature 038, and equivalently blocked specs 037 and 039 broader e2e rollups. Verified root cause via controlled reproduction (docker compose down --volumes -> up -> tight curl/psql probe loop): three contributing surfaces, all reproducible. (1) ./smackerel.sh up was invoking 'docker compose up -d' WITHOUT --wait, so the CLI returned as soon as containers were created/started, not when their healthchecks went green. (2) The postgres healthcheck used unsuffixed 'pg_isready -U $USER -d $DB' which connects via the unix socket. During initdb's bootstrap, postgres briefly exposes a temp server on the unix socket, then shuts it down to start the real TCP server. pg_isready could succeed against the temp server, container marked healthy, compose unblocks dependents via service_healthy, the test runs 'docker compose exec postgres psql ...', and hits postgres mid-shutdown emitting 'FATAL: the database system is shutting down'. (3) tests/e2e/lib/helpers.sh::e2e_wait_healthy only required 'curl -sf $CORE_URL/api/health' to return any 2xx, but /api/health returns 200 with {"status":"degraded"} when core itself cannot reach postgres or has degraded subsystems (e.g. unwired connectors). Fix (minimum viable, three coordinated edits, no new defaults/fallbacks): (a) docker-compose.yml postgres healthcheck switched to 'pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER} -d ${POSTGRES_DB} -h localhost -p 5432 -q' (forces TCP, defeating the unix-socket initdb false positive) plus start_period: 15s and retries: 10. (b) smackerel.sh up command now passes '--wait --wait-timeout 180' to docker compose up -d. (c) tests/e2e/lib/helpers.sh::e2e_wait_healthy now requires HTTP 200 from /api/health AND a successful 'psql -tAc SELECT 1' round-trip against postgres before returning, and surfaces last http_code on timeout for diagnostics. (d) tests/e2e/run_all.sh Phase 1 boot replaced inline curl /api/health wait with a call to the now-hardened e2e_wait_healthy so both phases use the same gating logic. Files modified (4): docker-compose.yml, smackerel.sh, tests/e2e/lib/helpers.sh, tests/e2e/run_all.sh. Verification (single full e2e run, no retries): ./smackerel.sh test e2e Phase 2 lifecycle 3/3 PASS - test_compose_start (SCN-002-001) PASS, test_persistence (SCN-002-004) PASS with 'Insert verified (count=1)' and 'PASS: SCN-002-004 (data persisted, count=1)', test_config_fail (SCN-002-044) PASS. Phase 1 shared 28/30 PASS. Both routed as separate findings to bubbles.workflow for assignment.
